#labels Phase-Deploy
#Platform specific installation instructions.

_mapero is in an early development phase so at the moment you only can test the application downloading the svn repository code and run it locally (we have not platform specifics package to install)_

*mapero* is based on an excellent python tool suit from [http://www.enthought.com/ Enthought Inc.], so basically the installation process is based on get running some components of ETS ([https://svn.enthought.com/enthought/wiki/InstallWithEggs Installing ETS using eggs]) and downloading the mapero svn code.

Here is resumed the process :

= Ubuntu Feisty =
  * Installing the ETS prerequisites
    {{{
    sudo apt-get install python2.5 python-wxgtk2.6 python-setuptools python-numpy python-scipy python-vtk 
    }}}
  * Installing the ETS required components
    {{{
    cd /usr/lib/python2.5/site-packages
    sudo wget http://code.enthought.com/enstaller/eggs/ubuntu/feisty/unstable/numpy.egg-info
    sudo easy_install -f http://code.enthought.com/enstaller/eggs/ubuntu/feisty/unstable enthought.chaco2
    }}}
  * Downloading the svn mapero code to your <desired-dir>
    {{{
    cd <desired-dir>
    svn checkout http://mapero.googlecode.com/svn/trunk/ mapero
    }}}
  * Running mapero dataflow editor
    {{{
    cd mapero/src
    python dvdataflow_editor_app.py
    }}}

= Windows XP/Vista =
  * Download and install a Python distribution from http://www.python.org/ftp/python/2.5.1/python-2.5.1.msi (x86 version)
  * Download and install enstaller using http://code.enthought.com/enstaller/ez_enstaller_setup.py
  * Install mapero development version
    {{{
    enstaller -f http://mapero.googlecode.com/svn/trunk/dist mapero
    }}}
  * add the python's installation dir and Scripts to %PATH%
  * run mapero-dfe-script.pyw
